pr.title: Add `pre-commit` to `reqs/dev.in` and update documentation, then format code
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Code-style/tooling PR that added pre-commit to the dev requirements, scoped the existing clang-format pre-commit hook to C/C++ source under src, updated CONTRIBUTING with pre-commit-based formatting instructions, regenerated reqs/dev.txt, and applied clang-format across 92 source/header files. The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 had "No changes", supporting that the large source churn was formatting-only for matching output purposes.

key_files.note: Auto Formatting section changed from git clang-format guidance to pre-commit workflow and says clang-format should run on all C code in src.
clang-format hook kept at v18.1.8 and given files regex ^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$.
Added pre-commit to the input dev dependency list.
Regenerated pinned dev requirements, including pre-commit==4.6.0 and transitive dependencies such as cfgv, identify, nodeenv, pyyaml, and virtualenv.
Largest formatting churn in the PR: 1001 additions and 580 deletions.
Large clang-format reflow in stage code.
Large clang-format reflow in game-mode code.
Representative formatter changes: long calls split, switch/goto label indentation adjusted, casts spaced.
Diff also drops #include "ef/efdata.h"; no PR discussion explains this beyond the cleanup/formatting context.

what_changed: Regenerated `reqs/dev.txt`; the header changed from pip-compile with Python 3.11/`--strip-extras` to Python 3.12/`--output-file=reqs/dev.txt`, and the lock now includes `pre-commit==4.6.0` plus its dependencies.
Updated `.pre-commit-config.yaml` so the clang-format hook only targets `^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$`.
Rewrote the CONTRIBUTING auto-formatting instructions to recommend installing dev packages, running the pre-commit hook, and running `pre-commit run --all-files` on existing code.

Applied clang-format-style mechanical reflow to 92 source/header files with 3778 added and 3374 deleted lines, especially under `src/melee/mn`, `src/melee/gm`, `src/melee/gr`, `src/melee/ef`, `src/melee/ft`, `src/melee/it`, and `src/melee/lb`.
The bot comment reported `Report for GALE01 ... No changes`, indicating the formatting sweep did not alter the built target output.
smart_moves: The tooling change was made in the full dependency path: source requirement (`reqs/dev.in`), compiled lockfile (`reqs/dev.txt`), hook config, and contributor docs were all updated together.
The hook was narrowly scoped to `src` C/C++/header extensions, reducing the risk that pre-commit would format unrelated files when contributors run it on all files.
The initial formatting sweep was bundled with the hook rollout, so future PRs can be held to the automated style without every contributor rediscovering old unformatted regions.
Keeping this as a code-style PR made the decomp.dev `No changes` report meaningful: reviewers can treat the huge diff as mechanical rather than as a matching or behavior change.
decomp_lessons: For decomp repositories, introduce formatting automation in a dedicated PR and verify with the project’s binary/matching bot; here GALE01 reported no output changes.
When a hook changes formatting expectations, update both `reqs/dev.in` and the generated lockfile so contributors get the same tool through documented setup commands.
Use a path/extension filter for formatting hooks; broad all-file hooks can create unrelated churn in generated files, assets, or non-C sources.

Large clang-format diffs may reflow long function calls, prototypes, casts, macro-heavy expressions, switch cases, and labels without changing semantics; future matching work should not infer behavior from this PR’s mechanical line changes.
Avoid mixing meaningful decomp work, naming, or new matches into a repo-wide formatting sweep unless the diff and bot output make those semantics explicit.
naming_conventions: No new symbol names or decomp naming conventions were introduced by this PR.
The CONTRIBUTING diff context still shows the existing guidance: avoid naming a function that you are not matching; this PR did not alter that rule.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level or matching tactic was introduced; the relevant matching evidence is the decomp.dev bot comment saying GALE01 had `No changes`.
The reusable tactic is process-oriented: isolate mechanical formatting, then rely on the build/matching report to prove no binary output change.
Formatting touched headers and prototypes, but the PR provides no evidence that those edits were intended to affect matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in the slice.
The only issue comment is from decomp-dev[bot]: `Report for GALE01 ... No changes`.
follow_up_queries: Look up issue #1543 for the original formatting/pre-commit motivation.
When reviewing later formatting diffs, compare against this PR as the baseline clang-format sweep.
Check future PRs that modify `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, clang-format rev v18.1.8, or the `^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$` hook scope.
Search for post-2463 PRs where decomp.dev reports changes despite formatting-only claims.
